title: HTTP Semantics, Roles, Representations, and Layered Evidence · Browser and Node Client Systems · Compiler Case: Client Server Roles
summary: An accessible operations dashboard calls a browser API through service workers and gateways, but one visible error collapses CORS, network, status, and schema failures. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts client server roles increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch protocol evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish client, server, user agent, origin, intermediary, gateway, proxy, and cache roles per exchange. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
